svg.title: Human Mesh Network Architecture
svg.desc: A layered diagram showing the four layers of the Human Mesh project: Transport, Data, Security, and Application logic.

g.text: Gossip protocol · Priority queue · TTL hop count

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the anti-entropy gossip protocol in detail')
sendPrompt('How does the TTL hop count prevent infinite loops in the mesh?')
sendPrompt('How should the priority queue rank text vs image data packets?')
sendPrompt('How does content hashing work for mesh data packets?')
sendPrompt('Explain Merkle trees and why they are useful for syncing mesh data')
sendPrompt('How does content-addressed deduplication prevent duplicate data in the mesh?')
sendPrompt('How can zero-knowledge proofs verify mesh membership without revealing identity?')
sendPrompt('How does packet obfuscation make mesh traffic look like normal BLE noise?')
sendPrompt('What encryption scheme should I use for mesh packet payloads?')

g.text: BLE advertising · Wi-Fi Direct · Device discovery

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does BLE advertising work for broadcasting 31-byte mesh packets?')
sendPrompt('How does Wi-Fi Direct work for peer discovery in the mesh?')
sendPrompt('What is the best Flutter plugin for BLE or nearby device discovery?')

svg.text: Tap any component to go deeper. Build order: bottom → top.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a simple first BLE Hello World I can build in Flutter?')
sendPrompt('What should I learn about Merkle trees before implementing mesh sync?')
sendPrompt('How do I stress test 30 simultaneous BLE devices for collision handling?')
sendPrompt('What security should I add to a mesh network and in what order?')
